Package i5.cb.graph

Class DiagramDesktop

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.JLayeredPane
            • javax.swing.JDesktopPane
              • i5.cb.graph.DiagramDesktop
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.awt.image.ImageObserver, java.awt.print.Printable, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener, java.util.EventListener

    public class DiagramDesktop
extends javax.swing.JDesktopPane
implements java.awt.print.Printable, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener, java.awt.image.ImageObserver
    Description of the Class
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class Description
      class  DiagramDesktop.DDMouseListener
      This inner class enables a user to mark more than one node as selected at at a time.

        java.awt.Component.AccessibleAWTComponent, java.awt.Component.BaselineResizeBehavior, java.awt.Component.BltBufferStrategy, java.awt.Component.FlipBufferStrategy

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field Description
      static java.lang.Integer BACKGROUND_LAYER
      Layer for background
      static java.lang.Integer EDGE_LAYER
      Layer for edges
      static int HAS_IMAGE
      Mask to check whether an image shall be saved/loaded; stored in bit 0 of an int number
      static int HAS_PARAMS
      Mask to check whether CBGraph was started with special parameters (e.g.
      static int HAS_SOURCE
      Mask to check whether the module source shall be saved/loaded; stored in bit 1 of an int number currently unused
      static java.lang.Integer NODE_LAYER
      Layer for nodes
      static int SELECTION_MODE_ADD
      In this selection mode the actual selection is cleared and all chosen nodes are selected.
      static int SELECTION_MODE_NEW
      In this selection mode the chosen nodes are added to the actual selection.
      static int SELECTION_MODE_SWITCH
      In this selection mode the chosen nodes are removed from the actual selection, if they are already selected.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addDiagramEdge​(DiagramEdge edgeToAdd, DiagramNode parent, int iPos)
      Adds a single DiagramEdgeto this diagramDesktop
      void addDiagramEdges​(java.util.Vector edgesToAdd, DiagramNode parent, int iPos, boolean bCompact)
      Adds diagramEdges to this diagramDesktop.
      void addDiagramNode​(DiagramNode node)
      Adds a DiagramNode to the desktop together with its DiagramEdge (if it has one)
      void addDiagramNode​(DiagramNode nodeToAdd, DiagramNode otherNode, int iPos)
      Adds a DiagramNode to the north, east, south or west of a certain other node
      void addDiagramNodes​(java.util.Collection nodesToAdd, DiagramNode parent, int iPos, boolean bCompact)  
      void addPropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
      Adds a PropertyChangeListener to the listener list.
      void addPropertyChangeListener​(java.lang.String prop, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)  
      void adjustContentPaneSize​()  
      void clearSelectedNodes​()
      Unselects all nodes that might be selected.
      boolean correctLocation​(DiagramNode node)
      Checks a DiagramNodewheter it is still inside this desktop's bounds.
      java.awt.Color getBackgroundColorAt​(java.awt.Point p, DiagramObject dobj)  
      java.util.Vector getContainedDiagramNodes​(DiagramNode cnode)
      Gets this DiagramDesktop's DiagramNodes (excluding the nodes on Edges) that are geometrically contained in the container node cnode
      DiagramNode getContainerNode​(DiagramNode dn)
      Gets the DiagramNode that is containing dn as component, returns null if not existent; this is the reverse function of getContainedDiagramNodes
      java.util.Vector getDiagramEdges​(boolean nodeRepresentation)
      Returns all diagramEdges shown on this desktop.
      java.util.Vector getDiagramNodes​()
      Gets this DiagramDesktop's DiagramNodes (including the nodes on Edges)
      GraphEditor getGraphEditor​()  
      GraphInternalFrame getGraphInternalFrame​()
      Gets this desktop's GraphInternalFrame
      static java.lang.String getImageUrl​(java.lang.String imageFilename)
      Expand a relative image filename to its absolute form.
      java.util.Vector getIncidentDiagramEdges​(DiagramObject dn)
      Returns all incident diagram edges of diagram object dn
      java.util.Collection getInvalidNodes​()  
      DiagramDesktopGLayouter getLayouter​()  
      boolean getMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge​()  
      java.util.Vector getNeighborhood​(DiagramNode dn)
      Returns the set of all neighbors and neighbors of neighbors of diagram node dn
      java.util.Vector getNeighbors​(DiagramNode dn)
      Returns the set of all direct neighbors of diagram node dn; this includes the nodes on edges incident to dn
      java.util.Vector getProperDiagramNodes​()
      Gets this DiagramDesktop's proper DiagramNodes (excluding the nodes on Edges)
      java.util.Collection getSelectedNodes​()
      Gets the selectedNodes attribute of the DiagramDesktop object
      float getZoom​()  
      boolean isEdited​()
      True if the graph in this diagram desktop has been edited, e.g.
      boolean isInSelectedGroup​(DiagramNode dn)
      Tells if a node and at least one other node are selected.
      boolean isScreenshotTaken​()
      True if the method saveScreenShot is currently active; controls the way how node shapes are painted
      void load​(java.io.ObjectInputStream in)
      Clears the DiagramDesktop and then restores a saved layout from the given ObjectInput.
      void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g)
      calls the JDesktopPane's paint method.
      void paintComponent​(java.awt.Graphics g)
      calls the JDesktopPane's paintComponent method.
      int print​(java.awt.Graphics printGraphics, java.awt.print.PageFormat pageFormat, int pageIndex)
      Prints this diagramDesktop
      void propertyChange​(java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent evt)  
      void redrawEdges​()
      Redraw all edges of this DiagramDesktop
      void removeDiagramObject​(DiagramObject dobj)
      Removes a DiagramObjectfrom the desktop.
      void removeInvalidNodes​()
      Removes the nodes that are considered invalid in some sense.
      void removeMarkedNodes​()
      Removes the nodes which are currently marked.
      void removePropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
      Removes a PropertyChangeListener from the listener list.
      void removePropertyChangeListener​(java.lang.String prop, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
      Removes a PropertyChangeListener from the listener list for the given property.
      void resizeDesktop​(int width, int height)
      Resize desktop to given values
      void save​(java.io.ObjectOutputStream out)
      Stores the position of all nodes, and which edges must be displayed.
      void selectAll​()
      Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop in selection mode 'new'.
      void selectAllEdges​()
      Selects all node on the edges of this DiagramDesktop in selection mode 'new'.
      void selectAllNodes​()
      Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop, that are not on an edge, in selection mode 'new'.
      void selectContainedNodes​(DiagramNode cnode)
      Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop that are geometrically contained in cnode; cnode is a diagram node with nodelevel below 0, hence used as a graphical container
      void setBackgroundImage​(java.lang.String imageFilename)  
      void setEdgesStraight​(DiagramNode dn)
      Straighten all edges that are incident to dn
      void setEdited​(boolean newvalue)
      Set edited status of this diagram desktop.
      void setInvalidNodesMethod​(java.lang.String method)  
      void setMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge​(boolean value)
      sets movability of nodes that sit on edges, i.e.
      void setNodeSelected​(DiagramNode node, boolean bIsSelected)  
      void setNodeSelected​(DiagramNode node, boolean bIsSelected, boolean bIsLeftClick)
      Sets a certain DiagramNodeto be treated as selected or not.
      void setSquareDots​(boolean visible)
      Control whether the square dot symbolizing the node on an edge with empty labels shall be visible; initially such square dots are not visible (opaque=false).
      void setUseSmoothLines​(boolean newUseSmoothLines)  
      void setZoom​(float z)  
      void toggleFrozen​(DiagramNode dn)
      Freeze / Unfreeze a diagram node's position
      boolean useSmoothLines​()  
      void validateEdges​()
      Validate the positions of all edges on this diagram desktop; they may be incorrect after moving a node whose links have links themselves

    • Field Detail

      • SELECTION_MODE_NEW

        public static final int SELECTION_MODE_NEW
        In this selection mode the chosen nodes are added to the actual selection.
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • SELECTION_MODE_SWITCH

        public static final int SELECTION_MODE_SWITCH
        In this selection mode the chosen nodes are removed from the actual selection, if they are already selected. Otherwise they are added.
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • SELECTION_MODE_ADD

        public static final int SELECTION_MODE_ADD
        In this selection mode the actual selection is cleared and all chosen nodes are selected.
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • HAS_IMAGE

        public static final int HAS_IMAGE
        Mask to check whether an image shall be saved/loaded; stored in bit 0 of an int number
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • HAS_SOURCE

        public static final int HAS_SOURCE
        Mask to check whether the module source shall be saved/loaded; stored in bit 1 of an int number currently unused
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

      • HAS_PARAMS

        public static final int HAS_PARAMS
        Mask to check whether CBGraph was started with special parameters (e.g. on synchronizing with the CBserver)
        See Also:
        Constant Field Values

        Layer for background

    • Constructor Detail

      • DiagramDesktop

        public DiagramDesktop​(GraphInternalFrame frame)
        Creates a new DiagramDesktop.
        Parameters:
        frame - the frame, this DiagramDesktop is associated to

    • Method Detail

      • resizeDesktop

        public void resizeDesktop​(int width,
                          int height)
        Resize desktop to given values

      • getGraphEditor

        public GraphEditor getGraphEditor​()

      • addDiagramEdge

        public void addDiagramEdge​(DiagramEdge edgeToAdd,
                           DiagramNode parent,
                           int iPos)
        Adds a single DiagramEdgeto this diagramDesktop
        Parameters:
        edgeToAdd - The diagramEdge we want to add.
        parent - The edge's source- or destination node
        iPos - one the N_POSITION, E_POSITION, S_POSITION or W_POSITION as defined in GEConstants. Defines the edges position relative to 'parent'.

      • addDiagramEdges

        public void addDiagramEdges​(java.util.Vector edgesToAdd,
                            DiagramNode parent,
                            int iPos,
                            boolean bCompact)
        Adds diagramEdges to this diagramDesktop. First extracts the nodes that are peeers of 'parent' and those that are sitting of the edges (the two groups apart from each other). Than adds the nodes with two calls of addDiagramNode(Collection, DiagramNode, int, boolean). Finally adds the diagramEdges themselfes.
        Parameters:
        edgesToAdd - a Vector containing the edges we want to add
        parent - a DiagramNode all new edges are incident to
        iPos - the new edges' relative position (i.e. north, west, south or east to parent)
        bCompact - tells if the new edges' endnodes (i.e. the peers of parent) shall be placed in a compact way or one a the lower right of another

      • addDiagramNodes

        public void addDiagramNodes​(java.util.Collection nodesToAdd,
                            DiagramNode parent,
                            int iPos,
                            boolean bCompact)
        Parameters:
        nodesToAdd - the nodes we shall add. It's assumed that all nodes are siblings
        parent - a single node beeing already on the diagramDesktop, somehow related to the nodesToAdd
        iPos - tells if the new nodes are to be added in the south, east, north or west of as seen from othernode
        bCompact - tells if only one axis (x or y) shall be altered or both.

      • addDiagramNode

        public void addDiagramNode​(DiagramNode node)
        Adds a DiagramNode to the desktop together with its DiagramEdge (if it has one)

      • addDiagramNode

        public void addDiagramNode​(DiagramNode nodeToAdd,
                           DiagramNode otherNode,
                           int iPos)
        Adds a DiagramNode to the north, east, south or west of a certain other node

      • removeDiagramObject

        public void removeDiagramObject​(DiagramObject dobj)
        Removes a DiagramObjectfrom the desktop. Also calls the diagramObject's erase() method
        Parameters:
        dobj - The DiagramObject to be removed.

      • removeMarkedNodes

        public void removeMarkedNodes​()
        Removes the nodes which are currently marked.

      • removeInvalidNodes

        public void removeInvalidNodes​()
        Removes the nodes that are considered invalid in some sense.

      • getDiagramNodes

        public java.util.Vector getDiagramNodes​()
        Gets this DiagramDesktop's DiagramNodes (including the nodes on Edges)
        Returns:
        a Vector containing the DiagramNodes residing in this DiagramDesptop
        See Also:
        DiagramNode

      • getProperDiagramNodes

        public java.util.Vector getProperDiagramNodes​()
        Gets this DiagramDesktop's proper DiagramNodes (excluding the nodes on Edges)
        Returns:
        a Vector containing the proper DiagramNodes residing in this DiagramDesptop
        See Also:
        DiagramNode

      • getContainedDiagramNodes

        public java.util.Vector getContainedDiagramNodes​(DiagramNode cnode)
        Gets this DiagramDesktop's DiagramNodes (excluding the nodes on Edges) that are geometrically contained in the container node cnode
        Parameters:
        cnode - the container node
        Returns:
        a Vector with all proper DiagramNodes geometrically contained in cnode
        See Also:
        DiagramNode

      • getContainerNode

        public DiagramNode getContainerNode​(DiagramNode dn)
        Gets the DiagramNode that is containing dn as component, returns null if not existent; this is the reverse function of getContainedDiagramNodes
        Parameters:
        dn - a DiagramNode of this DiagramDesktop
        Returns:
        the container that contains dn
        See Also:
        DiagramNode

      • getDiagramEdges

        public java.util.Vector getDiagramEdges​(boolean nodeRepresentation)
        Returns all diagramEdges shown on this desktop. You can choose whether to retrieve the DiagramEdges or the DiagramNodes by setting the nodeRepresentation parameter.
        Parameters:
        nodeRepresentation - flag to indicate whether to return the DiagramEdges or the DiagramNodes on the edges
        Returns:
        the DiagramEdges if nodeRepresentation is false. The DiagramNodes on the edges else.

      • getIncidentDiagramEdges

        public java.util.Vector getIncidentDiagramEdges​(DiagramObject dn)
        Returns all incident diagram edges of diagram object dn

      • getNeighborhood

        public java.util.Vector getNeighborhood​(DiagramNode dn)
        Returns the set of all neighbors and neighbors of neighbors of diagram node dn

      • getNeighbors

        public java.util.Vector getNeighbors​(DiagramNode dn)
        Returns the set of all direct neighbors of diagram node dn; this includes the nodes on edges incident to dn

      • save

        public void save​(java.io.ObjectOutputStream out)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Stores the position of all nodes, and which edges must be displayed. At the moment information about the shape of the edges(if they are bent) is lost. Keep in mind that there will not happen very much, if the GraphInternalFramedoes not implement the saveUserObject Method.
        Parameters:
        out - any ObjectOutput
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an error occurs

      • load

        public void load​(java.io.ObjectInputStream in)
          throws java.io.IOException,
                 java.lang.ClassNotFoundException
        Clears the DiagramDesktop and then restores a saved layout from the given ObjectInput. Will only work, if this DiagramDesktop is associated to a GraphInternalFrame. Keep in mind that there will not happen very much, if the GraphInternalFramedoes not implement the loadUserObject Method.
        Parameters:
        in - any ObjectInput where a DiagramDesktop has been stored
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an error occurs
        java.lang.ClassNotFoundException - if class String or Rectangle is not found, deserialization fails

      • getGraphInternalFrame

        public GraphInternalFrame getGraphInternalFrame​()
        Gets this desktop's GraphInternalFrame
        Returns:
        the Frame, this DiagramDesktop is associated to

      • getInvalidNodes

        public java.util.Collection getInvalidNodes​()

      • paintComponent

        public void paintComponent​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        calls the JDesktopPane's paintComponent method. We use it here to draw the background image of the palette (if defined via CBFrame)
        Overrides:
        paintComponent in class javax.swing.JComponent
        Parameters:
        g - a Graphics value

      • paint

        public void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        calls the JDesktopPane's paint method. Then it shows black rectangles around every marked node.
        Overrides:
        paint in class javax.swing.JLayeredPane
        Parameters:
        g - a Graphics value

      • correctLocation

        public boolean correctLocation​(DiagramNode node)
        Checks a DiagramNodewheter it is still inside this desktop's bounds. Changes the node's location if it isn't.
        Parameters:
        node - the diagramNode we want to check for.
        Returns:
        true if the location of the node had to be changed, false otherwise

      • getMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge

        public boolean getMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge​()
        Returns:
        true if diagram nodes that sit on edges shall move synchronously if one side of the edge is moved

      • setMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge

        public void setMovableDiagramNodeOnEdge​(boolean value)
        sets movability of nodes that sit on edges, i.e. when true the node representing the "middle" of the edge moves synchronously when one side of the edge is moved. This is the normal behaviour. If set to false, then the "middle" of the edge (where the edge label is shown) is fixed. This behaviour is useful when changing the palette without wanting the edges to be relocated.
        Parameters:
        value - the new value for movability of the nodes on edges.

      • getZoom

        public float getZoom​()

      • setZoom

        public void setZoom​(float z)

      • adjustContentPaneSize

        public void adjustContentPaneSize​()

      • print

        public int print​(java.awt.Graphics printGraphics,
                 java.awt.print.PageFormat pageFormat,
                 int pageIndex)
          throws java.awt.print.PrinterException
        Prints this diagramDesktop
        Specified by:
        print in interface java.awt.print.Printable
        Parameters:
        printGraphics - Description of the Parameter
        pageFormat - Description of the Parameter
        pageIndex - Description of the Parameter
        Returns:
        Description of the Return Value
        Throws:
        java.awt.print.PrinterException - Description of the Exception

      • isInSelectedGroup

        public boolean isInSelectedGroup​(DiagramNode dn)
        Tells if a node and at least one other node are selected.
        Parameters:
        dn - the node we want to know about
        Returns:
        true if the node is selcted on this desktop and if he is not the only selected one

      • setNodeSelected

        public void setNodeSelected​(DiagramNode node,
                            boolean bIsSelected,
                            boolean bIsLeftClick)
        Sets a certain DiagramNodeto be treated as selected or not. A node will be shown with a rectangular frame around it if it is selected.
        Parameters:
        node - a DiagramNode value
        bIsSelected - a boolean value
        bIsLeftClick - a boolean value

      • setNodeSelected

        public void setNodeSelected​(DiagramNode node,
                            boolean bIsSelected)

      • clearSelectedNodes

        public void clearSelectedNodes​()
        Unselects all nodes that might be selected.

      • selectAll

        public void selectAll​()
        Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop in selection mode 'new'.

      • selectAllNodes

        public void selectAllNodes​()
        Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop, that are not on an edge, in selection mode 'new'.

      • selectContainedNodes

        public void selectContainedNodes​(DiagramNode cnode)
        Selects all nodes of this DiagramDesktop that are geometrically contained in cnode; cnode is a diagram node with nodelevel below 0, hence used as a graphical container

      • selectAllEdges

        public void selectAllEdges​()
        Selects all node on the edges of this DiagramDesktop in selection mode 'new'.

      • getSelectedNodes

        public java.util.Collection getSelectedNodes​()
        Gets the selectedNodes attribute of the DiagramDesktop object
        Returns:
        The selectedNodes value

        public void propertyChange​(java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        Specified by:
        propertyChange in interface java.beans.PropertyChangeListener

      • addPropertyChangeListener

                                      java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
        Overrides:
        addPropertyChangeListener in class java.awt.Container

      • addPropertyChangeListener

        public void addPropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
        Adds a PropertyChangeListener to the listener list.
        Overrides:
        addPropertyChangeListener in class java.awt.Container
        Parameters:
        l - The listener to add.

                                         java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)

      • setInvalidNodesMethod

        public void setInvalidNodesMethod​(java.lang.String method)

      • useSmoothLines

        public boolean useSmoothLines​()

      • setUseSmoothLines

        public void setUseSmoothLines​(boolean newUseSmoothLines)

      • getLayouter

        public DiagramDesktopGLayouter getLayouter​()

      • getImageUrl

        public static java.lang.String getImageUrl​(java.lang.String imageFilename)
        Expand a relative image filename to its absolute form. I particulr check whether the image is locally stored in the CBICONS subdirectory of CB_HOME.

      • setBackgroundImage

        public void setBackgroundImage​(java.lang.String imageFilename)
        Parameters:
        imageFilename - the filename of the image (gif,png,jpg)

      • getBackgroundColorAt

        public java.awt.Color getBackgroundColorAt​(java.awt.Point p,
                                           DiagramObject dobj)

      • validateEdges

        public void validateEdges​()
        Validate the positions of all edges on this diagram desktop; they may be incorrect after moving a node whose links have links themselves

      • redrawEdges

        public void redrawEdges​()
        Redraw all edges of this DiagramDesktop

      • setEdgesStraight

        public void setEdgesStraight​(DiagramNode dn)
        Straighten all edges that are incident to dn

      • toggleFrozen

        public void toggleFrozen​(DiagramNode dn)
        Freeze / Unfreeze a diagram node's position

      • setSquareDots

        public void setSquareDots​(boolean visible)
        Control whether the square dot symbolizing the node on an edge with empty labels shall be visible; initially such square dots are not visible (opaque=false). When a node is selected on the diagram desktop then the square dots are made visible; when no more nodes are selected (e.g. by clicking in the background) then the square dots are made invisible

      • isEdited

        public boolean isEdited​()
        True if the graph in this diagram desktop has been edited, e.g. by adding, removing, or moving objects

      • setEdited

        public void setEdited​(boolean newvalue)
        Set edited status of this diagram desktop. Used for deciding whether the content has to be saved in a graph file

      • isScreenshotTaken

        public boolean isScreenshotTaken​()
        True if the method saveScreenShot is currently active; controls the way how node shapes are painted
